title: libertia insignis
intro: Libertia insignis, also known as the New Zealand iris, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and eastern Australia, characterized by its striking white and green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponin content. The health benefits of Libertia insignis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and its ability to support immune function. Herbal preparations of Libertia insignis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
